_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Sunday March 8th | 5:31

Bonjour Tremblant,

On this spring Sunday, we're expecting temperatures hovering around 0°C, bringing thick fog blankets this morning. Visibility will be affected on the mountain, so exercise extra caution as conditions can vary significantly from one area to another and throughout the day. Moderate winds and very high humidity complete the picture. Make sure to wear warm and waterproof layers for your day on the mountain!

Note that several trails, including the terrain parks, will have delayed openings this morning. This allows us to assess conditions in detail and ensure your safety. Our team is working tirelessly to offer you the best conditions possible! Moreover, while alpine touring trails are mostly closed, with the exception of the Nansen, Algonquin, and Beauchemin ascent trails which remain open for those seeking variety.

First Tracks: Access to designated lifts on both North and South sides as early as 7:45 a.m., with skiing on North Side slopes at 8 a.m.

Enjoy your day on the mountain and ski safely!

Mountain safety tip of the week: Slow down in reduced‑speed zones.
Reduced‑speed areas mean it’s time to slow down for your safety and the safety of others.
Remember, you’re sharing the slopes with other skiers and riders. Stay cautious at all times.
Some zones marked in yellow on the trail map require reduced speed. Please be considerate, and ski or ride slowly in these areas out of respect for the higher traffic.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Bonjour Tremblant! Sunday brings spring conditions with temperatures around 0°C, thick fog, and reduced visibility. Moderate winds and high humidity require warm, waterproof layers. Several trails and terrain parks open late this morning as our team assesses conditions for your safety. Alpine touring mostly closed except Nansen, Algonquin, and Beauchemin ascent trails. Ski safely!
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Dimanche apporte des conditions printanières avec des températures autour de 0°C, du brouillard épais et une visibilité réduite. Des vents modérés et une humidité très élevée demandent des couches chaudes et imperméables. Plusieurs pistes et parcs à neige ouvrent progressivement ce matin. Randonnée alpine majoritairement fermée sauf les pistes de Nansen, Algonquin et Beauchemin. Skiez prudemment!
